mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations where UNRATE remains below 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in commercial and industrial credit (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a healthy demand-supply equilibrium that expands corporate profit margins, lifting equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for SPX 60-day returns (p > 0.15) or flips sign incorrectly across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices above a 2.5 percent annualized rate triggers inflation expectations that compress real yields, initially boosting cyclical equity valuations before monetary policy reacts.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds 2.5 percent while FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly increases in real GDP reflect underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth versus SPX 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly employment growth exceeding 200 thousand signals robust labor demand, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as market sentiment shifts toward optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling unemployment indicates structural labor demand outpacing supply, creating inflationary pressure that initially lifts cyclical equities over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of HOUST and UNRATE shows no predictive power for SPX 20-day returns (p > 0.15) or fails to produce positive returns when HOUST rises and UNRATE falls.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent while unemployment exceeds 6.0 percent, the economy enters a liquidity trap where additional monetary accommodation fails to stimulate growth, depressing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or neutral in more than 60 percent of instances meeting the FEDFUNDS and UNRATE thresholds.
